{
  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 0,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Untwisted pairs at the termination exceed the maximum allowable limit of 0.5 inches (13 mm) for Category 5e/6/6A cabling."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-terminate the data cable ensuring that the pair twists are maintained as close as possible to the point of termination, not exceeding 0.5 inches."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to TIA-568 standards, maintaining the twist of the pairs up to the point of termination is critical to minimize near-end crosstalk (NEXT) and ensure signal integrity. For Category 5e and higher, the maximum allowable untwisted length at termination is 0.5 inches (13 mm). Exceeding this limit degrades network performance and fails compliance.",
  "references": [
    "ANSI/TIA-568-C.2",
    "NEC Article 800"
  ]
}
